authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. aerospace production-approval framework that governs quality-record and production-control obligations relevant to release-ready reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA guidance defines practical production, documentation, and supplier-control expectations that shape aerospace analytics source assumptions.
used_for: source precedence for production and quality records

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative aerospace quality framework for evidence quality, nonconformance handling, and terminology used in reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral supply-chain operating reference for supplier controls, flowdown handling, and lifecycle traceability expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7007, Contractor Counterfeit Electronic Part Detection and Avoidance System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public clause that directly drives supplier-source qualification and reporting posture for suspected non-conforming materials.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7008, Sources of Electronic Parts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Controls expected source hierarchy for electronic parts and informs when supplier changes must be routed to procurement and supplier owners.

authoritative_sources.title: NASA-STD-8739.6, Implementation Requirements for NASA Workmanship Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral aerospace workmanship public standard for quality and evidence handling expectations in manufacturing analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 46.407, Nonconforming supplies or services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal nonconformance baseline for acceptance, rejection, and disposition boundaries relevant to aerospace quality-release reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify terminology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry identifier standard for stable lot, batch, and serial linkage across enterprise sources.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public disclosure standard for uncertainty statements and narrative limits when analysis becomes audience-facing.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 3, Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized control baseline for handling controlled records in analysis and reporting contexts, including telemetry and evidence handling.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect tenant facts, systems of record, hold status, lot genealogy, and supplier evidence; validate freshness and authority lineage before metric calculation.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and denominator alignment
workflow_stages.description: Define metric definitions, exclusions, and denominator rules with explicit tenant and source precedence.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware analysis assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble analysis from ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and supplier evidence while preserving conflicts and unresolved traceability ties.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify terminology
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Release and quality-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unresolved holds, NCRs, MRB impacts, and substitution pressure that exceed this lane's reporting authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure and conflict resolution
workflow_stages.description: When claims or policies are unsupported, issue escalation, refusal, or blocked-confidence outputs rather than overconfident reporting.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: Production orders, work orders, lot and serial genealogy records
ERP, MES, WMS inventory and movement states
quality hold records, NCRs, MRB status, and change-control records
supplier master data, C of C packages, supplier corrective actions, and source qualification artifacts
release records, hold authorizations, and gate artifacts
throughput, escape-rate, and supplier-quality trend inputs

allowed: Classify in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked analytics requests.
Define metric logic, source precedence, and evidence lineage for aerospace reporting.
Prepare analytical packages with confidence, traceability, and actionable recommendations.
Escalate when hold, nonconformance, supplier substitution, or release boundary implications are present.
escalate: Any unresolved lot genealogy conflict between ERP, MES, and WMS.
Missing or stale evidence affecting release posture, supplier quality, or throughput claims.
Requests that would require supplier substitution, engineering rework acceptance, or hold clearance decisions.
Missing tenant policy context that changes the legal, release, or supplier boundary.
refuse: Requests for legal, financial, licensing, release, safety-critical engineering, or supplier-approval decisions.
Claims framed as causal, benchmark, or risk-certainty statements beyond evidence support.
Bypassing traceability, hold, or CUI/disclosure controls in order to publish outputs.

domain_failure_modes: Treating finance and operations throughput denominators as interchangeable without explicit tenant contract.
Publishing hold-sensitive release posture while NCR or MRB state is unresolved.
Collapsing ERP, MES, and WMS lot records into one line item without lineage notes.
Approving supplier substitution or source changes from analytics output alone.
Overstating trend causality from correlated aerospace defect or supplier metrics.
Sharing controlled supplier or manufacturing records without disclosure/risk checks.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define baseline aerospace hold behavior; tenant SOP and customer program rules determine the effective release or exception thresholds per facility and lot.
Release, MRB, engineering, and procurement authority paths are tenant-specific and must be supplied at request time before conclusions are finalized.
Public guidance identifies disclosure quality expectations, while specific tenant/external signoff requirements must be provided per audience.

notes: Research set includes manufacturing quality, supplier traceability, and hold/quality references required for the aerospace manufacturing-and-supply-chain domain pack.
Source refresh and decay policy are explicit and can be checked against tenant retrieval timestamps at runtime.
